edges.relation: Legendre symbol constructs Jacobsthal matrix which IS the conference matrix
Conference matrix serves as input A in GS array; bordering is a special case of GS
GS circulant condition IS the complementary sequence PAF=0 condition
PAF flatness in time domain ↔ PSD flatness in frequency domain (DFT duality)
PSD flatness objective can be encoded as QUBO cost Hamiltonian
Truncated Kronecker product yields modular Hadamard approximation
Williamson matrices are a special case (symmetric circulant) of GS input matrices
Cyclotomic classes provide structured difference sets in Z_p
QR/QNR are the 2-class cyclotomic partition; Paley uses this directly
SDS directly yield circulant matrices for the GS array
T-matrices plug into Cooper-Wallis variant of GS construction
GF(167)* = Z_166 = Z_2 × Z_83 determines available cyclotomic structures
GF(167) provides the finite field for Legendre symbol and Jacobsthal matrix
Complementary set condition and flat PSD are DFT-dual formulations
No set of four ±1 sequences of length 167 with flat total PSD = 668 is known
Proposed path: quantum advantage may solve instances classical computers cannot

bridge_chains.name: Number Theory → Signal Processing → Quantum Computing
bridge_chains.description: From quadratic residues in GF(167) through conference matrices and GS array to spectral flatness condition, then to quantum optimization. Crosses number theory, combinatorial design, signal processing, and quantum computing.

bridge_chains.name: Group Theory → Combinatorics → Optimization
bridge_chains.description: From the algebraic structure of Z_167* through cyclotomic partitions and difference sets to the GS array and spectral optimization. Crosses algebra, combinatorics, and optimization.

bridge_chains.name: Linear Algebra → Coding Theory → Open Problem
bridge_chains.description: From Kronecker product construction through modular relaxation to the open problem. Shows how exact constructions (H(672)) yield approximations (4-modular H(668)).

bridge_chains.name: Algebraic Combinatorics → Signal Design → Information Theory
bridge_chains.description: From Williamson matrix constraints through sequence design to information-theoretic optimality conditions. Bridges discrete algebra and continuous signal theory.
